methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $4.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.5B - $39.0B.

data.narrative:
Luma AI, an AI & Software company founded by Amit Jain and Alex Yu, is a Series C-stage private firm currently valued at $4.0B post-money, having raised $1.07B to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private peers and 1 public company (Palantir, ticker PLTR), with four same-sector, same-stage private comparables providing direct benchmarking: Figure AI at $39.0B, Skild AI at $14.0B, and Runway and Magic AI each at $1.5B.

Two methodologies were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $6.8B. Peer-median valuation across the four same-stage, same-sector comparables produces $7.8B, with the peer set exhibiting substantial dispersion (range: $1.5B to $39.0B). This wide spread reflects heterogeneity in market positioning and investor confidence within the AI software cohort at Series C.

Triangulating these two methods yields a low estimate of $5.8B, median of $7.3B, and high of $8.9B. The current $4.0B valuation sits below the triangulated range, suggesting potential upside to $7.3B median. Key uncertainties include the drivers of the wide peer dispersion—particularly whether Figure AI's $39.0B reflects comparable fundamentals—and the appropriate step-up multiplier, which varies materially across market conditions and company-specific risk profiles.
